node_or_relation: ADA4932_2 outputs connect toward AD9269 H/L clamp diode area through downstream resistors
observed_value: red arrows point to AD9269 H/L clamp diodes
interpretation: ADA4932_2 remains the immediate driver of the abnormal clamp-side nodes.

node_or_relation: ADA4932_1 is upstream of ADA4932_2 through resistors and relays
interpretation: The two same-model chips are cascaded rather than independent symmetric references.
Upstream stage is also rail-side. ADA4932_2 may be responding to an upstream forced differential condition rather than being the only damaged part.
Downstream stage directly drives clamp conduction and AD9269-side abnormality when R61/R62 are connected.

node_or_relation: ADA4932_1 to ADA4932_2 via resistors and relay contacts
observed_value: not yet mapped by exact endpoints
interpretation: Next decision depends on isolating whether ADA4932_2 saturation is driven through this interstage path or caused by ADA4932_2/local feedback itself.

node_or_relation: ADA4932_1 Pin10/Pin11 to ADA4932_2 Pin3/Pin2 through 100R-relay-100R-817R paths
observed_value: confirmed conductive with about 3 mA to 3.7 mA estimated current
interpretation: Interstage path is active and normally conductive in the measured state; current priority shifts upstream to ADA4932_1 and its input/relay selection.

node_or_relation: B8A / AD8512 outputs upstream of ADA4932_1
observed_value: Pin1 and Pin7 outputs both about 1.26 V
interpretation: B8A is upstream but is not rail-side at DC; next split is the B8A-to-ADA4932_1 network versus ADA4932_1 local failure.

node_or_relation: B8A Pin7 to ADA4932_1 Pin3 through 50R and about 1.457k
observed_value: Pin3 held at -0.45 V with about 1 mA estimated path current
interpretation: Current fault focus is ADA4932_1 Pin3 local network or ADA4932_1 internal damage; B8A output remains near 1.26 V.
